items.audit_item: All features at time t use only information available at or before t
items.notes: Chronological split and lagged features only.

items.audit_item: The 3-day-ahead target is never used in feature scaling
items.notes: Scaling is fit on the training subset only and excludes the target.

items.audit_item: Scaling parameters are fitted on training data only
Rolling volatility uses only past/current observations
items.notes: Rolling features are built with lag/shift operations.

items.audit_item: Rolling sentiment aggregation uses only past/current sentiment values
items.notes: Sentiment smoothing is causal within each token series.

items.audit_item: Graph construction is performed using training-period data only for each split
items.notes: Graph helper consumes the training panel only; features are causal.

items.audit_item: MIS selection is computed using training-period graph only
items.notes: Selected on the graph returned from training-period data.

items.audit_item: PRISM V3 uses meta-learning-inspired adaptation proxy, not strict MAML
items.notes: V3 is an MLPRegressor with anchor blending (0.7*price + 0.3*pred), honestly labelled as adaptation proxy in all outputs and manuscript inserts.

items.audit_item: Validation data are not used in meta-training updates except for tuning/early stopping
items.notes: Validation is used only for model selection and early stopping.

items.audit_item: Test data are never used for feature selection, token selection, graph construction, scaling, tuning, or early stopping
items.notes: Test rows are held out before tuning and scaling.

items.audit_item: All ablation variants use the same token subset, same split, same horizon, and same target transformation
items.notes: Ablation variants are derived from the same processed panel with identical splits.

items.audit_item: Target normalization, if used, is inverted consistently for all models and variants
items.notes: No inverse scaling mismatch is introduced in the revision pipeline.
